react.answer:
## Building a Speed Dial Menu in React

**Mental model:** A floating action button (FAB) that toggles a stack of action buttons. One `open` boolean controls everything; the actions animate in (translate + fade) with a per-item stagger delay. Close on action click and on outside click.

### Step 1 — Open state + FAB
```jsx
const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);
<button onClick={() => setOpen((o) => !o)} style={{ transform: open ? 'rotate(45deg)' : 'none' }}>+</button>
```

### Step 2 — Render the action stack
Map actions above the FAB; only interactive when open.

```jsx
{actions.map((a, i) => (
  <button style={{ transitionDelay: `${i * 40}ms`, opacity: open ? 1 : 0, transform: open ? 'translateY(0)' : 'translateY(10px)', pointerEvents: open ? 'auto' : 'none' }} />
))}
```

### Step 3 — Stagger via transition-delay
The `i * 40ms` delay makes items cascade out; CSS `transition` does the motion.

### Step 4 — Close on action or outside click
```jsx
const run = (a) => { a.onClick(); setOpen(false); };
useEffect(() => { /* document mousedown closes when outside */ }, []);
```

**Edge cases**

| Case | Handling |
| :-- | :-- |
| Click action | Run then close. |
| Outside click | Close via document listener. |
| Keep mounted | Hide with opacity/pointer-events (don't unmount) so the exit animates. |

**Interview tip:** Keeping the actions mounted and toggling `opacity`/`pointer-events` (instead of conditionally rendering) is what lets both the enter *and* exit transitions play — a subtle but important animation detail.

vue.answer:
## Building a Speed Dial Menu in Vue 3

**Mental model:** A FAB toggles a stack of action buttons. One `open` boolean controls everything; actions animate in with a per-item stagger via `transition-delay`. Close on action click and outside click.

### Step 1 — Open state + FAB
```vue
<button @click="open = !open" :style="{ transform: open ? 'rotate(45deg)' : 'none' }">+</button>
```

### Step 2 — Action stack
```vue
<button v-for="(a, i) in actions" :key="a.label" :style="itemStyle(i)" @click="run(a)">{{ a.icon }}</button>
```

### Step 3 — Stagger
```js
const itemStyle = (i) => ({
  transitionDelay: (open.value ? i : actions.length - i) * 40 + 'ms',
  opacity: open.value ? 1 : 0,
  transform: open.value ? 'translateY(0)' : 'translateY(12px)',
  pointerEvents: open.value ? 'auto' : 'none',
});
```

### Step 4 — Close on action / outside
```js
function run(a) { a.onClick(); open.value = false; }
```

**Edge cases**

| Case | Handling |
| :-- | :-- |
| Click action | Run then close. |
| Outside click | Document listener. |
| Exit animation | Keep mounted; toggle opacity/pointer-events. |

**Interview tip:** Toggling `opacity`/`pointer-events` instead of `v-if` keeps the items mounted so both enter and exit transitions play — the same trick applies in any framework.

angular.answer:
## Building a Speed Dial Menu in Angular

**Mental model:** A FAB toggles a stack of action buttons. One `open` boolean controls it; actions animate with a per-item stagger via `transition-delay`. Close on action click and outside click. Classic `NgModule` setup, no `?.`/`??` (the build rejects them).

### Step 1 — Open state + FAB
```html
<button (click)="open = !open" [style.transform]="open ? 'rotate(45deg)' : 'none'">+</button>
```

### Step 2 — Action stack
```html
<button *ngFor="let a of actions; let i = index" [ngStyle]="itemStyle(i)" (click)="run(a)">{{ a.icon }}</button>
```

### Step 3 — Stagger (method returns a style object)
```typescript
itemStyle(i: number): Record<string, string> {
  return {
    'transition-delay': (this.open ? i : this.actions.length - i) * 40 + 'ms',
    opacity: this.open ? '1' : '0',
    transform: this.open ? 'translateY(0)' : 'translateY(12px)',
    'pointer-events': this.open ? 'auto' : 'none',
  };
}
```

### Step 4 — Close on action / outside
```typescript
run(a: Action): void { a.onClick(); this.open = false; }
@HostListener('document:click', ['$event']) onDoc(e: MouseEvent): void { if (!this.host.nativeElement.contains(e.target)) { this.open = false; } }
```

**Edge cases**

| Case | Handling |
| :-- | :-- |
| Click action | Run then close. |
| Outside click | `@HostListener` + `contains`. |
| Exit animation | Keep mounted; toggle opacity/pointer-events. |

**Interview tip:** `[ngStyle]` with a method returning the per-item style object is the clean way to express the staggered transition in Angular — and keeping items mounted (not `*ngIf`) preserves the exit animation.
